project.title: Tandem mass spectrometry proteotyping of extremophiles sampled from high-altitude Andean lakes
project.projectDescription: The proteome of the SS13 isolate assigned to the Chromatiaceae family was assessed by shotgun proteomics using a pan-proteomics database for the genus Rheinheimera and a whole genome sequencing -derived database.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein samples were loaded on SP3 beads, reduced and alkylated, digested by trypsin, and the resulting peptides were analyzed with a Q-Exactive HF tandem mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS/MS spectra were analyzed by MASCOT against a database comprising the protein sequences of all genome sequenced Rheinheimera or a database derived from SS13 genome sequencing. The results were then parsed, spectral counts were extracted and compared.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: CEA-Marcoule, ProGenoMIX platform, DRF-Li2D, 30207 Bagnols-sur-Ceze, France
labPIs.name: Jean Armengaud

references.referenceLine: Runzheimer K, Lozano C, Boy D, Boy J, Godoy R, Matus FJ, Engel D, Pavletic B, Leuko S, Armengaud J, Moeller R. Exploring Andean High-Altitude Lake Extremophiles through Advanced Proteotyping. J Proteome Res. 2024 23(3):891-904

Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: 0.1 μg of Trypsin Gold in 50 mM NH4HCO3
Comment[Instrument]: Q-Exactive HF (Thermo Scientific, Villebon sur Yvette, France)
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: data-dependent acquisition mode with a Top20 strategy
Comment[Separation]: nanoscale PepMap 100 C18 nanoLC column (3 μm, 100 Å, 75 μm i.d. × 50 cm, Thermo Scientific)
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: a maximum of two missed cleavages
